name: Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca
region_intro: Mountain biking around Pampelune offers a diverse landscape, ranging from the foothills of the Pyrenees to lush river valleys and semi-desert areas. The region of Navarra, where Pampelune is located, features an extensive network of paths suitable for bikes. Riders can explore varied terrain, including dense forests, open plains, and routes along the Arga River. This geographical diversity provides options for all skill levels, from gentle riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best mountain bike trails around Pampelune The most popular mountain bike trail is Pamplona - Zabalgain – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route incorporates the city's fluvial park, offering a mix of urban green spaces and natural scenery. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca, an easy 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This route explores the city's historic sites and the scenic river park, suitable for a relaxed ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Water Fountain and Low Wall – View from Monte Ezkaba loop from Iruñeko tren geltokia, a 16.5 miles (26.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views from Monte Ezkaba, often completed in about 2 hours. Mountain biking around Pampelune is defined by Pyrenees foothills, Arga River valleys, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside routes to more challenging climbs with significant elevation gain. The routes in Pampelune are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 380 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pampelune's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pampelune?
A: Pampelune and the surrounding Navarra region offer an extensive network of mountain bike trails. There are over 150 routes listed on komoot for the immediate Pampelune area, with the wider Navarra region boasting over 3,000 km of paths suitable for bikes, including hundreds of signposted routes for all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Pampelune?
A: The terrain around Pampelune is incredibly diverse. You'll find everything from the foothills of the Pyrenees with challenging ascents and descents, to lush river valleys along the Arga River, and even unique semi-desert landscapes like the Bardenas Reales. Routes often traverse dense forests, open plains, and incorporate urban green spaces.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Pampelune?
A: Yes, Pampelune offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990087617/tour-a-la-ciudadela-de-pamplona-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 9.1-mile (14.6 km) path that explores the city's historic sites and scenic river park. The Plazaola Greenway, an old railway route, also offers a wide, linear forest track suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region's proximity to the Pyrenees foothills provides more demanding routes. Areas like the Baztán Valley and Merindad de Pamplona are known for their strenuous mountain biking through forests and mountains. Routes with significant elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989955673/pamplona-zabalgain-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Zabalgain – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca&lt;/a&gt;, offer substantial climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Pampelune?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Pampelune are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989955673/pamplona-zabalgain-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Zabalgain – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990087617/tour-a-la-ciudadela-de-pamplona-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona – circular por el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca&lt;/a&gt;, both incorporating the city's fluvial park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24667749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Fountain and Low Wall – View from Monte Ezkaba loop from Iruñeko tren geltokia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain biking around Pampelune offers access to various landmarks and natural features. You can encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4627298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel of Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4112457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidelus Mendi&lt;/a&gt;. The Arga River Park itself offers extensive green spaces and river views.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by castles?
A: Yes, some routes will bring you close to historical castles and fortifications. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990087617/tour-a-la-ciudadela-de-pamplona-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the city's historic sites, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4627298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel of Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;. The wider region also features other castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2707420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irulegi Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pampelune?
A: The mountain bike trails around Pampelune are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 380 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the well-maintained paths along the Arga River, and the variety of options available for different skill levels, from scenic urban routes to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Is it possible to find mountain bike trails with cafes or pubs nearby?
A: Many trails, especially those closer to or passing through Pampelune and its surrounding towns, will have cafes, pubs, or restaurants accessible. The routes that incorporate the city's fluvial park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990087617/tour-a-la-ciudadela-de-pamplona-circular-por-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour a la Ciudadela de Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly convenient for finding refreshments before, during, or after your ride.

Q: Are there dedicated facilities for mountain bikers in the Navarra region?
A: Yes, the Navarra region has invested in mountain biking infrastructure. There are 8 dedicated MTB spaces and 23 MTB reception centers that offer services such as maps, technical information, bike washing areas, repair stations, and changing rooms. These facilities are designed to enhance the mountain biking experience in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Pampelune?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for mountain biking in Pampelune, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the more exposed areas, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potential snow in higher elevations, particularly in the Pyrenees foothills. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking on trails in the Pampelune region?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on most public trails around Pampelune. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, especially in protected natural areas like the Bardenas Reales, where some areas may have seasonal restrictions or require specific access. For detailed information on regional regulations, you can consult the official tourism website for Navarra: &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/you-like/nature-based-tourism" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
This is the most important bridge of the four medieval ones that cross the Arga as it passes through Pamplona.

It was built in the twelfth century and later renovated in the nineteenth century. It consists of five large slightly pointed arches, triangular tajamares and semicircular discharge arches on the supports. It is related to that of Puente la Reina. A transept, probably built in the sixteenth century, points the traveler at one end of the bridge to the entrance of the Camino de Santiago to the city. In it you can see the image of the Jacobean saint.

After crossing this Romanesque bridge, the pilgrim arrives at the Tejería Park and from there to the walls of Pamplona, under the old city of Navarrería, which is accessed by the Portal de Francia.
